Overview Approach Research Design UI Design Thank You
Product Design, CMF, HMI

NTE
Generator

A construction-grade power generator, built to be the quietest, toughest, and easiest to use open-frame machine on the market. It pairs that hardware with an on-board interface anyone can read at a glance.

Concept to Final Design Rhino 3D and KeyShot Adobe XD and Photoshop Team of 3 designers
FIG.01 Industrial CAD RenderOpen-Frame
Scroll
#1
Quietest open-frame generator on the market
3
Core priorities: noise, quality, and ease of use
17
Competitors benchmarked across three tiers
4
On-board screens: Home, Service, Diagnostic, and Alerts
02. Process
The Approach

A three-phase path from study to concept.

The work moved from research into form exploration, then settled into a branded concept that was ready to build.

01
PHASE 01

Study

  • Input collection and study of competition and reference inputs
  • Mood board and theme board for high-level design direction and brainstorming
02
PHASE 02

Ideation

  • Form exploration and ideation
  • Initial concept, 3 to 4 directions
03
PHASE 03

Concept Design

  • Concept review and refinements
  • Concept imagery with branding and colors
03. Research
Voice of the Customer

Three demands shaped every decision.

We turned what customers cared about most into the selling points that anchored the brief.

Noise

VOC #1

Quietest line of commercial-grade open-frame generators on the market.

Sound suppression tech Orfeld labs partnership Super-quiet muffler

Quality

VOC #2

Toughest, most reliable commercial-grade generators on the market.

Honda GX engines Rugged heavy-duty frame Class-leading warranty

Ease of Use

VOC #3

Most intuitive and simple-to-operate open-frame generators available.

Centralized controls Push-button start Illuminated controls
04. Benchmark
Competitive Analysis

Positioned for a complete assortment.

We mapped seventeen players across a Good, Better, Best structure to find the gap NTE could own.

GOOD
Value tier
BETTER
Mainstream reliability
BEST
NTE, premium commercial

The landscape

Most brands cluster by how durable and premium they feel. NTE targets the Best tier, sitting alongside Honda, Generac XC and NorthStar, while staying open-frame and field-serviceable.

HondaGenerac XC NorthStarYamaha KohlerCAT Atlas CopcoWacker Neuson ChampionPredator DuroMaxFirman Powerhorseand more
05. Direction
Theme and Mood Board

Stealth, strength, endurance.

The visual language drew from car engines, stealth geometry, and rugged off-road equipment, turning "reliable, powerful, built to last" into form.

Faceted surfaces and dampening textures signal quiet; muscular framework and accents signal strength. A futuristic stance keeps it premium while staying honest and practical.

StealthQuietRuggedMuscularFuturistic
Mood keywords
Reliable
Powerful
Endurance
Texture
06. Ideation
Ideation Sketches

Exploring form, handles and controls.

We explored open-frame, modern silhouettes alongside pull handles, button and knob clusters, and backlit branding details.

07. Rendering
Digital Rendering

Resolving the machine, side by side.

Each face was worked out on its own: the engine, muffler, alternator, and control sides, with callouts for acoustic panels, cord management, and the lit logo.

08. Final Design
Final Design

A resolved, manufacturable form.

The final design shown in full orthographic and hero views: top, generator side, engine side, and three-quarter.

09. Interface
On-board Experience

The HMI and Display UI.

Beyond the hardware, NTE carries a smart display. It shows load, runtime, service intervals, and safety-critical alerts in an interface you can read at a glance, designed around the decisions operators actually make.

Home Cluster

The default view. Total load, estimated run time, and fuel level at a glance. The colour shifts from green to red as the machine nears its limit.

80%, 3.2H
90%, 2.2H
98%, 1.0H
101%, 0.2H

Service

Maintenance at a glance. It shows how much life each serviceable part has left, so downtime gets planned instead of discovered.

Spark Plug32 hrs left
Oil32 hrs left
Air Filter100 hrs left
CO Sensor2708 days left

Diagnostic

The full electrical picture for advanced operators: line voltages, frequency, and per-line amperage in one readout.

L1 and L2 voltage241 V
Frequency60 Hz
L1 and L2 voltage121 V, 120 V
L1 and L2 amperage30 A, 5 A

Alerts

Notifications that can appear on any screen. They escalate from a calm advisory up to a full red danger-shutdown warning.

Advisory, Low Fuel (30 min)
Advisory, Low Fuel (10%)
Service, Change Oil
Service, Oil + Filter
Service, Air Filter
Service, Spark Plug
Service, CO Sensor
Shutdown, Low Oil
Danger, CO Shutdown
HMI, original UI design, preserved as authored
10. Architecture
Information Architecture

Four screens, one clear decision path.

The interface is organised so operators always know the current status, what needs servicing, the full diagnostic picture, and any active danger, with no menus to learn.

SCREEN 01

Home

  • Fuel level
  • Battery
  • Volts and Watts
  • Total, L1 and L2 load
  • Hour meter
SCREEN 02

Service

  • Spark plug
  • Air filter
  • Oil
  • CO sensor life
SCREEN 03

Diagnostic

  • L1 and L2 voltage
  • L1 and L2 amperage
  • L1 and L2 voltage
  • Frequency ratio
CONDITIONAL

Alerts

  • On-alert pop-ups
  • Danger notifications
  • Actionable guidance
  • Appears across screens
11. Flow
User Story

From power-on to action.

A simple, predictable flow, with branches that only appear when something needs attention.

Welcome
Generator on
Home
Live widgets
Service
Next button
Diagnostic
Next button
Alert or Notification
If alert on any screen
End of Case Study

Thank you.

The NTE Generator brings industrial design, CMF, and HMI together into one product story, from the first sketch to the on-board interface.
